title: calystegia macrostegia subsp. cyclostegia
intro: Calystegia macrostegia subsp. cyclostegia, also known as Island False Bindweed, is a perennial plant native to the Channel Islands of California, characterized by its twining stems and trumpet-shaped flowers. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its antispasmodic, antitussive, and expectorant properties, which help to relieve coughs, bronchitis, and other respiratory issues. The health benefits of Calystegia macrostegia subsp. cyclostegia include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of health conditions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
